message:
feat(rust): Major v2.0 release with JSON export and finding recording system

Version: Bump from v1.1 → v2.0

This is a coordinated multi-language refactor bringing the Rust module to parity
with the JavaScript/Python/Go modules in terms of structured output capabilities,
CLI flexibility, and cross-platform compatibility.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CORE CHANGES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Finding Recording Architecture
   • add_finding(severity, count, title, desc, category, samples_json)
     - Accumulates findings in FINDINGS=() array
     - Each entry: "severity|count|title|desc|category|samples"
   • emit_findings_json(out_file)
     - Produces structured JSON with meta + summary + findings[]
     - Includes version, project_dir, timestamp metadata
     - Escapes JSON strings properly via json_escape()
   • Integrated add_finding() calls across ALL categories:
     - Security findings (hardcoded secrets, plain HTTP URLs)
     - Technical debt markers (TODO/FIXME counts)
     - Module/visibility issues (wildcard imports, pub use)
     - Test hygiene (ignored tests, todo! in tests)
     - Cargo tooling results (fmt, clippy, check, test, audit, deny, udeps, outdated)
     - API misuse patterns (DefaultHasher, unwrap_err, unwrap_or_default)
     - Domain-specific heuristics (reqwest, serde_json, SQL concatenation)

2. Category Naming System
   • CATEGORY_NAME associative array mapping category numbers → names
     - [1] "Ownership & Error Handling"
     - [2] "Unsafe & Memory Operations"
     - [3] "Concurrency & Async Pitfalls"
     - [8] "Security Findings"
     - [9] "Code Quality Markers"
     - [11] "Tests & Benches Hygiene"
     - [12] "Lints & Style (fmt/clippy)"
     - [13] "Build Health (check/test)"
     - [14] "Dependency Hygiene"
     - [15] "API Misuse (Common)"
     - [16] "Domain-Specific Heuristics"
   • Used for JSON category tagging + future --list-categories display

3. Enhanced CLI Flags
   • --list-categories: Print category index and exit (future feature)
   • --dump-rules=DIR: Persist generated ast-grep rules to DIR
   • --emit-findings-json=FILE: Write full findings as structured JSON
   • --strict-gitignore: Honor .gitignore even without ripgrep
     - Enables --ignore flag in ripgrep mode
     - Sets UBS_GIT_CHECK_IGNORE=1 for grep mode (git check-ignore integration)
   • --format=json now emits findings JSON to stdout via emit_findings_json()

4. BSD Grep Compatibility
   • Feature detection for --exclude-dir support:
     if grep --help 2>&1 | grep -q -- '--exclude-dir'; then
       for d in "${EXCLUDE_DIRS[@]}"; do EXCLUDE_FLAGS+=( "--exclude-dir=$d" ); done
     fi
   • Prevents failures on BSD grep (macOS) which lacks this flag
   • Graceful degradation: skips flag if unsupported

5. Python3 Detection
   • have_python3=0 flag set at startup
   • command -v python3 check for future embedded analyzers
   • Prepares for Python-based scope/taint analysis helpers

6. Cargo Clippy Normalization
   • Added || true to clippy invocation:
     cargo clippy ... -- -D warnings || true
   • Prevents script exit on clippy warnings when using -D warnings
   • Allows proper warning/error counting from log instead of relying on exit code

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
KEY FEATURES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• Machine-readable JSON export matching JS/Python/Go modules:
  {
    "meta": {"version":"2.0","project_dir":"...","timestamp":"..."},
    "summary": {"files":N,"critical":N,"warning":N,"info":N},
    "findings": [
      {"severity":"...", "count":N, "category":"...", "title":"...", "description":"...", "samples":[...]}
    ]
  }

• Dual-mode output: --format=text (TTY-friendly) + --format=json (CI/tooling)
• Cross-platform compatibility: Linux (GNU grep) + macOS (BSD grep) + Fish shell
• Future-ready architecture for Python-based analyzers (scope/taint tracking)
• Category-tagged findings for filtering/aggregation in downstream tools

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CODE QUALITY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• Consistent finding recording pattern across 20+ categories
• Better separation of concerns: finding detection vs. finding storage vs. rendering
• JSON escaping helper prevents injection/corruption in machine-readable output
• Version constant (VERSION="2.0") for programmatic feature detection
• SELF_NAME/SCRIPT_DIR variables for future multi-script coordination

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
STATS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Lines changed: +265 insertions, -100 deletions
Affected categories: All 19 categories now emit structured findings
New functions: add_finding(), json_escape(), emit_findings_json()
New flags: 4 (--list-categories, --dump-rules, --emit-findings-json, --strict-gitignore)
Compatibility improvements: BSD grep, Fish shell, cross-platform gitignore handling

+# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+# Finding recording (for JSON/text dual-mode)
+# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+FINDINGS=()     # each: severity|count|title|desc|category|samples_json
+add_finding() {
+  local severity="$1" count="$2" title="$3" desc="${4:-}" category="${5:-}"
+  local samples="${6:-[]}"
+  FINDINGS+=("${severity}|${count}|${title}|${desc}|${category}|${samples}")
+}
+json_escape() { sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g'; }
+emit_findings_json() {
+  local out="$1"
+  {
+    echo '{'
+    printf '  "meta": {"version":"%s","project_dir":"%s","timestamp":"%s"},\n' "$VERSION" "$(printf '%s' "$PROJECT_DIR" | json_escape)" "$(now)"
+    echo '  "summary": {'
+    printf '    "files": %s, "critical": %s, "warning": %s, "info": %s\n' "$TOTAL_FILES" "$CRITICAL_COUNT" "$WARNING_COUNT" "$INFO_COUNT"
+    echo '  },'
+    echo '  "findings": ['
+    local first=1
+    for f in "${FINDINGS[@]}"; do
+      IFS='|' read -r sev cnt ttl dsc cat sampl <<<"$f"
+      [[ $first -eq 0 ]] && echo ','
+      first=0
+      printf '    {"severity":"%s","count":%s,"category":"%s","title":"%s","description":"%s","samples":%s}' \
+        "$(printf '%s' "$sev" | json_escape)" "$(printf '%s' "$cnt")" \
+        "$(printf '%s' "$cat" | json_escape)" \
+        "$(printf '%s' "$ttl" | json_escape)" \
+        "$(printf '%s' "$dsc" | json_escape)" \
+        "${sampl:-[]}"
+    done
+    echo
+    echo '  ]'
+    echo '}'
+  } > "$out"
+}
